# UEFN Toolbelt

> Python automation framework for Unreal Editor for Fortnite (UEFN).
> Exposes 355 tools through a PySide6 dashboard, an editor top-menu, and an MCP HTTP bridge.
> Any MCP-compatible AI (Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf, Zed, OpenClaw, NemoClaw, etc.) can control UEFN directly.

## What this is

UEFN Toolbelt is an open-source (AGPL-3.0) tool suite for UEFN 40.00+ (March 2026).
It covers ~97% of the UEFN Python API surface. The remaining 3% is locked by Epic
(heightmap editing, Blueprint graph nodes, Verse compiler trigger, match control, Cloth/Hair sim).

Tools are registered with `@register_tool` and called via `tb.run("tool_name", **kwargs)`.
Every tool returns `{"status": "ok"/"error", ...data}` — zero None returns, zero log parsing needed.

## How to use

```python
# In the UEFN Python console — nuclear reload + launch dashboard
import sys; [sys.modules.pop(k) for k in list(sys.modules) if "UEFN_Toolbelt" in k]; import UEFN_Toolbelt as tb; tb.register_all_tools(); tb.launch_qt()

# Run any tool
tb.run("world_state_export")
tb.run("scatter_hism", asset_path="/Engine/BasicShapes/Cube", count=200, radius=3000)
tb.run("verse_template_deploy", name="elimination_scoring", filename="game.verse", overwrite=True)
```

## MCP bridge (AI ↔ UEFN)

```python
# 1. Start listener in UEFN
tb.run("mcp_start")   # → http://127.0.0.1:8765

# 2. .mcp.json is pre-configured in this repo — any MCP client auto-connects
```

Once connected, an AI agent can call all 355 tools, spawn/move/delete actors,
execute arbitrary Python with full `unreal.*` access, and read structured results.

## Key files for AI agents

- `CLAUDE.md` — full codebase context, every tool table, mandatory rules
- `docs/PIPELINE.md` — 6-phase autonomous game-building pipeline
- `docs/UEFN_QUIRKS.md` — non-obvious UEFN Python behaviors (crashes, thread lock, mount detection)
- `docs/ui_style_guide.md` — mandatory for any PySide6 UI work
- `TOOL_STATUS.md` — per-tool test coverage status
- `ARCHITECTURE.md` — system design, directory map, data flow
- `.agents/workflows/` — step-by-step agent workflows (add_new_tool, run_tests)
- `scripts/drift_check.py` — validates version/tool-count consistency across all docs

## Tool categories (54 total)

Lighting · Post-Process · Audio · Materials · Procedural/Layout · Bulk Operations ·
Advanced Alignment · Actor Organization · Zone Tools · Proximity Placement · Level Stamps ·
Foliage/Scatter · LOD & Optimization · Nanite · UV Channels · DataTable · Textures ·
Skeletal Mesh · Curves · Blueprints · Landscape · Sound Assets · Asset Management ·
Reference Auditor · Level Snapshot · Asset Tagger · Viewport Navigation · Visibility & Lock ·
Selection Sets · Screenshot · Text & Signs · Verse Tools · Project Scaffold · API Explorer ·
Config · Plugin Management · Level Health · Entities · Environmental · Generative ·
Pipeline · Project Admin · Selection · Sequencer · Simulation · System · MCP Bridge ·
Measurement · Localization · DataTable · World State · Device Catalog · Schema Utils

## Mandatory rules for AI agents

1. **Never commit without a live UEFN test** — syntax checks do not catch runtime failures.
2. **Always run `deploy.bat` before testing** — repo and UEFN project are separate directories.
3. **Run `python scripts/drift_check.py`** before every commit to catch stale tool counts.
4. **Bump `__tool_count__` and `__category_count__`** in `Content/Python/UEFN_Toolbelt/__init__.py` when adding tools.
5. **Nuclear reload is unsafe for new modules** — use full UEFN restart when adding a new entry to `tools/__init__.py`. See UEFN_QUIRKS.md Quirk #26.
6. **All PySide6 windows must subclass `ToolbeltWindow`** — see `docs/ui_style_guide.md`.
7. **Check the tool registry before adding anything** — 355 tools exist; search first.
